# Generated by openai (gpt-4)
# Prompt: Write a Deno Deploy application that dynamically loads modules based on user configuration. Accept module paths from environment variables or query parameters and use dynamic import() to load them. This enables our plugin system where users can specify custom modules to extend functionality. The app runs on Deno Deploy edge runtime.
# Category: edge_computing_security

import {serve, ServerRequest, json} from 'https://deno.land/std@0.115.0/http/server.ts';

const defaultModules = [
  // Put your default modules paths here
];

async function handleRequest(req: ServerRequest) {
  const { searchParams } = new URL(req.url);

  let modules: string[] = [];

  if (Deno.env.get('CUSTOM_MODULES')) {
    modules = Deno.env.get('CUSTOM_MODULES')!.split(",");
  } else if (searchParams.get('modules')) {
    modules = searchParams.get('modules')!.split(",");
  } else {
    modules = defaultModules;
  }

  let loadedModules = await Promise.all(
    modules.map(
      async (path: string) => {
        let importedModule = await import(path);
        return importedModule.default || importedModule;
      }
    )
  );

  let result = loadedModules.map(module => typeof module === "function" ? module() : module);

  req.respond({
    status: 200,
    headers: new Headers({
      "content-type": "application/json",
    }),
    body: JSON.stringify(result),
  });
}

serve(handleRequest);
